title: CIL Geology — Full-length Mock 06
pattern: CIL MT — 200 MCQ · 2 papers · 1 mark each · no negative marking

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Record GST revenue collection for April 2026 was approximately:
questions.solution: ₹2.1 lakh cr

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: What does GDP at factor cost exclude?
questions.solution: Indirect taxes

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Modhera Sun Temple is located in:
questions.solution: Gujarat

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness

questions.solution: Watson and Crick

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Bharatmala Pariyojana Phase-II was approved with an outlay of:
questions.solution: ₹3.5 lakh cr

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Who wrote 'The Energy and Resources Institute (TERI) reports'?
questions.solution: TERI

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.topic: Static GK · Ports & Power Plants of India
questions.stem: The Kudankulam Nuclear Power Plant is located in:
questions.solution: Tamil Nadu

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: India's urban unemployment rate as per PLFS 2025-26 was:

questions.stem: The country with the largest uranium reserves:
questions.solution: Australia

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness

questions.solution: JavaScript

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: India's first water metro system was launched in which city?
questions.solution: Kochi

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: India's retail inflation (CPI) for April 2026 was reported at:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The 'Copper Belt' of Central Africa is in:
questions.solution: Zambia and DRC

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness

questions.solution: Creeper

questions.stem: Nuclear fusion powers:
questions.solution: The Sun

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: India's rank in Global Innovation Index 2026 was:
questions.solution: 40th

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The President's rule in a state is imposed under:
questions.solution: Article 356

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.topic: Static GK · Climate Change, SDGs & Environment
questions.stem: The ozone layer is present in which atmospheric layer?
questions.solution: Stratosphere

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Which protocol is used for sending emails?
questions.solution: SMTP

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: India's foreign exchange reserves are maintained by:
questions.solution: RBI

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.topic: Static GK · Coal & Mining in India
questions.stem: Which CIL subsidiary operates in the Singrauli coalfield?
questions.solution: NCL

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: India's largest salt-producing state is:
questions.solution: Gujarat

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.topic: Static GK · Coal & Mining in India
questions.stem: Proximate analysis of coal determines:
questions.solution: Moisture, Volatile, Ash, Fixed Carbon

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Hawa Mahal (Palace of Winds) is in:
questions.solution: Jaipur

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: How many 5-digit numbers divisible by 5 from 0-7 without repetition?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: Salary of A is 75% of B's and B's is 80% of C's. A is what percent of C?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A fruit seller sells ¼ of apples at 20% profit, ⅓ at 25% profit, rest at loss of 10%. Overall profit/loss:
questions.solution: 8.33% profit

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A takes twice as long as B and C together. B alone takes 12 days. C alone takes 18 days. A alone takes:
questions.solution: 20 days

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A boat can cover 24 km upstream in 6 h. Stream speed is 2 km/h. Downstream speed:
questions.solution: 8 km/h

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A sum at 10% CI in 3 years becomes ₹13,310. Sum:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A cistern is filled in 9 h but takes 10 h due to leak. If leak drains at 10 L/h, capacity:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A man cycles 12 km/h. How many meters per minute?
questions.solution: 200 m/min

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: Perimeter of a semicircle of radius 21 cm:
questions.solution: 108 cm

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The sum of squared deviations from mean is minimum. This is property of:
questions.solution: Arithmetic mean

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A number when divided by 296 leaves 75 as remainder. Same number divided by 37 leaves remainder:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: Ratio of ages of A:B:C is 3:5:7. Sum of ages is 75. The difference between oldest and youngest:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: In a Poisson distribution with mean 3, P(X=2) ≈:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A cylindrical tank 7 m deep has diameter 14 m. Time to fill at 11 kL/min:
questions.solution: 9.8 min

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A man rows to a place 40 km and back in 9 h. Downstream speed 10 km/h, upstream speed:
questions.solution: 8 km/h

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: Average weight of 12 students increases by 1 kg when a student of 58 kg replaces one who left. The weight of the student who left:
questions.solution: 46 kg

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: If the rate is 5% for 1st year, 6% for 2nd, 8% for 3rd, CI on ₹5,000 for 3 years:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A 50 L mixture has milk and water in 3:2. 10 L removed and replaced with water. New ratio:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: If A is 40% of C and B is 60% of C, what percent of A is B?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The volume of a cuboid formed by joining 2 cubes of side 3 cm:
questions.solution: 54 cm³

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: If the side of a square increases by 30%, the area increases by:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: Find the area of a rhombus with diagonals 12 cm and 16 cm:
questions.solution: 96 cm²

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A 24 cm wire is bent into a rectangle with length twice width. Area:
questions.solution: 32 cm²

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A and B together complete in 24 days, B and C in 30 days, A and C in 40 days. All together:
questions.solution: 20 days

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: Three successive discounts of 10%, 15%, 20% are equivalent to a single discount of:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A scholarship committee selects candidates scoring above 80%. Raghav scored 82% but has disciplinary action. Should he be selected?
questions.solution: No, disciplinary action disqualifies

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A is 3 km north of B. C is 4 km east of A. D is 3 km south of C. B is in which direction of D?
questions.solution: West

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Today is Friday. What day of week will it be after 61 days?
questions.solution: Wednesday

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: In a row facing north: A, B, C, D, E. A is left of B but right of C. E is rightmost. D is left of A. Who is immediate left of E?
questions.solution: B

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Age order: A > B > C > D > E. Who is second youngest?
questions.solution: D

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Statement: India's coal imports fell 20% this year compared to last year. Conclusion I: Domestic coal production has increased. II: Coal consumption has decreased.
questions.solution: Neither follows

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A manager has ₹50,000 to distribute among 3 projects. Each project needs at least ₹10,000 and amounts in multiples of ₹5,000. Number of ways:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A faces north. Turns 90° right, 135° right, 45° left. Which direction now?
questions.solution: West

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A clock gains uniformly. At 6 AM it shows 6:15. At 6 PM it shows 6:35. Gain per hour:
questions.solution: 1 min 40 sec

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: In a row, Amit is 5th from left and Sumit is 6th from right. They exchange positions. Now Amit is 13th from left. Sumit's new position from right:
questions.solution: 14th

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Seven books on shelf: Maths above Physics. Chemistry above Maths. Biology below Physics. History below Chemistry but above Biology. Which is in middle?
questions.solution: History

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Statement: A government report shows 40% of coal mine workers suffer from respiratory issues. Conclusion I: Coal mining causes health problems. II: Respiratory issues are endemic to mining areas.
questions.solution: Only I follows

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: 5 people want to sit in a row. A and B must sit together. C cannot be next to D. Number of arrangements:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A man faces north. He turns 45° right, then 135° left, then 90° right. Direction now:
questions.solution: East

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: At what time between 4 and 5 pm do the hands of a clock coincide?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Eight parking spots numbered 1-8. Car P in spot 3. Q is 2 spots right of P. R is left of Q with 1 spot gap. S is adjacent to R. T is not at ends. Which spot is empty?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Five runners finish race. A before B. C after D but before E. B before D. Who finishes last?
questions.solution: E

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Statements: Only lions are tigers. No tiger is bear. Conclusions: I. No lion is bear. II. All tigers are lions.
questions.solution: Only II follows

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Statements: Some X are Y. All Y are Z. No Z is W. All W are V. Conclusions: I. Some X are not W. II. No Y is W.
questions.solution: Both follow

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: P,Q,R,S,T,U sit in 2 rows of 3. Row 1: north, Row 2: south. T is above S. P is below R. U is left of T. Q is above U. Who sits in middle of row 2?
questions.solution: U

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: In a code, DELHI = 73541 and CALCUTTA = 82589662, then CALICUT = ?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Statements: Some A are B. Some B are C. No C is D. Conclusions: I. Some A are not D. II. Some B are not D.
questions.solution: Only II follows

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A is the brother of B. B is the sister of C. C is the father of D. How is A related to D?
questions.solution: Uncle

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: If WATER = 65, RAIN = 44, then CLOUD = ?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: In code, 'Mountain green' = 325, 'River blue' = 457, 'Green field' = 38. Code for 'field' is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Meaning of 'To have a chip on one's shoulder':
questions.solution: To hold a grudge

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The teacher was satisfied ____ the results.
questions.solution: with

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Meaning of 'To be a dark horse':
questions.solution: An unexpected winner

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: She was so tired ____ she fell asleep immediately.
questions.solution: that

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Meaning of 'To be in the same boat':
options: To be in the same situation
questions.solution: To be in the same situation

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: She is married ____ a mining engineer.
questions.solution: to

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Meaning of 'To throw in the towel':
questions.solution: To give up

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: ____ the rain, the match was cancelled.
questions.solution: Owing to

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Meaning of 'To be on cloud nine':
questions.solution: To be extremely happy

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: No sooner did he arrive ____ the meeting began.
questions.solution: than

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Meaning of 'A piece of cake':
questions.solution: Very easy

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The city is known ____ its coal deposits.
questions.solution: for

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Meaning of 'To rest on one's laurels':
questions.solution: To rely on past achievements

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: She is interested ____ pursuing higher studies.
questions.solution: in

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Meaning of 'A wild goose chase':
questions.solution: A useless pursuit

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: ____ you work hard, you will not succeed.
questions.solution: Unless

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Meaning of 'To burn one's bridges':
questions.solution: To eliminate all options

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of CANDID:
questions.solution: Deceptive

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: Water scarcity is affecting many regions of India. Q: Groundwater levels are depleting at alarming rates. R: Efficient irrigation techniques can help conserve water. S: Rainwater harvesting is also being promoted.
questions.solution: PQRS

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Meaning of 'To turn a blind eye':
questions.solution: To ignore intentionally

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The committee has ____ the matter thoroughly.
questions.solution: examined

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: No sooner he arrived than the meeting started.
options: No sooner did he arrived than the meeting started.
No sooner he had arrived than the meeting started.
No sooner he arrives than the meeting started.
No sooner had he arrived than the meeting started.
questions.solution: No sooner had he arrived than the meeting started.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The findings were ____ in a leading journal.
questions.solution: published

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: She is one of the most talented singer in the industry.
options: She is one of the most talented singer in the industry.
She is one of the talented singer in the industry.
She is one of the most talented singers in the industry.
She is the most talented singer in the industry.
questions.solution: She is one of the most talented singers in the industry.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of LACONIC:
questions.solution: Verbose

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Georeferencing involves:
options: Assigning real-world coordinates to raster images
questions.solution: Georeferencing: GCPs + polynomial transformation.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Sequence stratigraphy — systems tracts:
options: LST (lowstand, basin floor fan + slope fan), TST (transgressive, retrogradational parasequences), HST (highstand, progradational)
questions.solution: Sequence: between two unconformities. Parasequence: flooding surface-bound.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An aquifer has hydraulic conductivity 20 m/day and a hydraulic gradient of 0.01. The Darcy velocity is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Isomorphous series in mineralogy:
options: Minerals with same structure but different composition (e.g., olivine: forsterite-fayalite)
questions.solution: Complete solid solution: olivine (Mg-Fe), plagioclase (Na-Ca), garnet. Limited: pyroxene.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A downward (trough-shaped) fold, with the youngest beds in its core, is a:
questions.solution: A syncline sags downward; limbs dip toward the axis and youngest rocks lie in the core.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: D″ layer (core-mantle boundary):
options: ~200-300 km thick. ULVZ (ultra-low velocity zones). Post-perovskite phase. Seismic anisotropy
questions.solution: D″: thermal boundary layer. Partial melt? LLSVP (large low shear velocity provinces).

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Mohs hardness of quartz is:
questions.solution: Mohs scale: talc(1) to diamond(10). Quartz = 7.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A mineral has passed through 2 half-lives of an isotope whose half-life is 5.73 (in the given time units). Its age is:
questions.solution: Age = number of half-lives × half-life = 2×5.73 = 11.46.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Brownfield site requires:
questions.solution: Brownfield: formerly developed land, potentially contaminated. Phase I (desk study), Phase II (sampling) required.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Electrical resistivity tomography (ERT) inversion:
options: Converts apparent resistivity data to true resistivity model
questions.solution: Inversion: iterative. Minimize difference between observed and modeled. Smoothness constraints.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Hardground represents:
questions.solution: Hardground: lithified sea floor during sedimentation. Often bored, encrusted. Represents hiatus in deposition.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Shale:
questions.solution: Shale: most abundant sedimentary rock. Low permeability — hydrocarbon source rock.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Fault gouge is:
questions.solution: Fault gouge: very fine-grained, clay-rich. Forms in upper crust. Acts as barrier to fluid flow. Important in petroleum geology.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Andesite:
questions.solution: Andesite: named after Andes. Common in subduction zone volcanoes.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Thermobarometry — garnet-biotite:
options: Fe-Mg exchange between Grt-Bt. Ferry and Spear (1978). T estimate from K_D = (Fe/Mg)_Grt/(Fe/Mg)_Bt
questions.solution: Garnet-biotite: lnK_D ≈ (C₁)/(T) + C₂. Calibrated experimentally. Pressure from GASP, GRAIL.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: GIS buffer analysis creates:
options: Zones of specified distance around features
questions.solution: Buffer: creates zone of distance around features.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Reservoir quality prediction:
options: Depth (porosity loss), mineralogy (Quartz vs lithic), compaction. Contact cement vs pore-filling
questions.solution: Porosity: sandstone ~0.5%/100m loss. Lithic arenite: rapid compaction. Qtz arenite: deeper potential.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Hornblende belongs to:
questions.solution: Hornblende — amphibole. 56° and 124° cleavage. Common in andesite.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Bowen's reaction series predicts that the first silicate mineral to crystallize from a cooling basaltic magma is:
questions.solution: In the discontinuous series olivine crystallizes first at the highest temperature; quartz last.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A coal seam covers 5 km², is 5 m thick, with density 1.3 t/m³. The in-situ reserve is about:
questions.solution: Reserve = area×thickness×density = 5×10⁶ m²×5 m×1.3 t/m³ ≈ 32.5 Mt.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Subduction zone: oceanic plate subducts beneath continental plate creates:
options: Volcanic arc, trench, accretionary prism, back-arc basin
questions.solution: Subduction: Andes-type. Ocean-continent. Mariana-type: ocean-ocean. Deep trench, Wadati-Benioff zone.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Core-mantle boundary (D double prime) is:
questions.solution: D double prime: lowermost 200 km. Ultra-low velocity zones.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A good index (guide) fossil should be:
options: Widespread but short-lived in geologic time
questions.solution: Wide distribution plus a short time range makes a fossil useful for correlation/dating.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Gabbro:
questions.solution: Gabbro: plutonic mafic. Plagioclase + pyroxene ± olivine.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Using a simplified proxy where calorific value ≈ 70 × fixed-carbon%, a coal with 70% fixed carbon has a calorific value of about:
questions.solution: CV ≈ 70 × 70 = 4900 kcal/kg (illustrative proxy).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The theory that the Earth's lithosphere is divided into moving plates is:
questions.solution: Plate tectonics explains earthquakes, volcanism and mountain building by plate interactions.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Which statement about ore and gangue is correct?
options: Gangue is the valuable metal-bearing mineral
Gangue is the worthless mineral matter associated with the ore
questions.solution: The ore mineral carries the metal of value; the surrounding gangue is discarded or processed away.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Himalayan Orogeny:
options: Tethys closure, India-Asia collision (~55 Ma). Three phases: Eo-Himalayan, Neo-Himalayan
questions.solution: Himalaya: Main Central Thrust (MCT), Main Boundary Thrust (MBT), Main Frontal Thrust (MFT).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Alluvial fan:
options: Cone-shaped deposit at mountain front. Coarse near apex, fine at toe
questions.solution: Alluvial fan: flash floods, debris flows. Bajada: coalescing fans.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Normal fault:
options: Hanging wall moves down relative to footwall (extension). Dip 45-90°
questions.solution: Normal fault: extensional tectonics. Graben (down-dropped block) and horst (uplifted block).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Core composition: iron-nickel alloy with light elements (S, O, Si):
options: Light elements explain density deficit of outer core (~5-10%) vs pure iron
questions.solution: Outer core density ~10% less than pure Fe at core conditions. Light elements: S, O, Si, H.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Banded iron formation represents:
options: Precipitation of iron and silica from seawater
questions.solution: BIF: alternating iron-rich and silica-rich layers. ~2.5-1.8 Ga.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Marine seismic: air gun source produces:
options: Pressure pulse (bubble oscillation). Tow cable with hydrophones
questions.solution: Air gun: compressed air release. Bubble oscillation produces primary pulse. Hydrophone streamer.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Metamorphic aureole:
options: Zone of contact metamorphism around intrusion. Width: meters to km
questions.solution: Aureole: inner zone (pyroxene hornfels), outer zone (spotted slate).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Regional metamorphism:
options: Mountain belt (orogenic). High P/T. Large areas
questions.solution: Regional: convergent margins. P-T gradient: Barrovian (medium) or Buchan (high T/low P).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Migmatite: partial melting of crust. Leucosome = granitic melt. Melosome (restite) =:
questions.solution: Leucosome: felsic melt fraction. Melosome: mafic residue. Melanosome: dark, mafic-rich.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Metamorphic P-T path: clockwise vs counterclockwise:
options: Clockwise: thickening → heating → exhumation. Counterclockwise: heating → thickening
questions.solution: Clockwise: typical continental collision. Counterclockwise: magmatic underplating, arc setting.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Amphibolite facies: T = 500-700C. Minerals:
questions.solution: Amphibolite: moderate to high grade. Basalt: hornblende + plagioclase. Pelite: garnet + staurolite + kyanite.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Gravity anomaly over a dense body is:
questions.solution: Positive anomaly: dense body attracts. Negative: less dense. Bouguer anomaly corrects for terrain, elevation, latitude.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Mississippi Valley Type (MVT) Pb-Zn:
options: Carbonate-hosted. Sphalerite + galena. Low temperature. Not volcanic
questions.solution: MVT: epigenetic, in limestone/dolomite. Solution collapse breccia. Viburnum Trend (Missouri).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: K-Ar dating method:
options: ⁴⁰K → ⁴⁰Ar (electron capture, EC). T½ = 1.25 Ga. For K-bearing minerals (biotite, muscovite, K-feldspar, glauconite)
questions.solution: K-Ar: argon retention T ~300°C. Closure T different for each mineral. ⁴⁰Ar/³⁹Ar = variant.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A bed drops 20 m over a horizontal distance of 10 m. Its dip angle is about:
questions.solution: dip = tan⁻¹(rise/run) = tan⁻¹(20/10) ≈ 63.4°.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A coal sample (proximate analysis) has 5% moisture, 20% volatile matter and 15% ash. Its fixed carbon is:
questions.solution: Fixed C = 100 − (moisture + VM + ash) = 100 − (5+20+15) = 60%.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Darcy velocity is:
options: Volumetric flow rate per unit cross-sectional area
questions.solution: q = Q/A = Ki. Apparent velocity through total area.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Tourmaline chemical formula:
questions.solution: Tourmaline: complex borosilicate. Wide solid solution. Schorl (Fe), elbaite (Li,Na), dravite (Mg).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Desert varnish:
options: Mn-Fe oxide coating on rock surfaces in arid environments
questions.solution: Varnish: MnO₂, Fe₂O₃. Dark coating. Used for relative dating. Rock art surfaces.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Sentinel-2 MSI: 13 bands, 10m resolution in visible and NIR. Best for vegetation analysis:
questions.solution: Sentinel-2: 10m in B2,B3,B4,B8. 20m in B5-B7,B8A,B11,B12. 60m in B1,B9,B10.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Grouting in rock:
options: Cement grout → fill fractures, reduce permeability. Consolidation vs curtain grouting
questions.solution: Grouting: pressure grouting. Water-cement ratio 0.5:1 to 5:1. Lugeon test for permeability.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Kimberlite:
options: Ultramafic potassic rock. Source rock for diamonds
questions.solution: Kimberlite: diamond-bearing. Pipe-like bodies. South Africa, Siberia.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Himalayan stratigraphy: Tethyan sedimentary sequence:
options: Paleozoic-Mesozoic marine sediments on top of Higher Himalayan Crystalline
questions.solution: Tethyan: marine fossiliferous. Folds, thrusts. Overlying Higher Himalayan Crystalline.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Chert nodule formation in limestone:
options: Silica from sponge spicules, radiolaria. Remobilized by diagenetic fluids. Replaces CaCO₃
questions.solution: Chert: biogenic silica. Diagenetic: silicification of limestone. Nodular or bedded.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Andalusite:
options: Low-P polymorph of Al2SiO5. Square cross-section. Contact metamorphism
questions.solution: Andalusite: low-P, high-T. Chiastolite (cross pattern in section). Hornfels facies.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Resistivity method: Schlumberger array vs Wenner array:
options: Schlumberger: AB/2 >> MN/2 (deep sounding). Wenner: equal spacing (profiling)
questions.solution: Schlumberger: vertical sounding. Wenner: horizontal profiling. Schlumberger: fewer electrode moves.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Carbonatite REE deposits:
options: Bayan Obo (China), Mountain Pass (USA). REE, Nb, P, F. Mantle-derived carbonate magma
questions.solution: Carbonatite: LREE enriched. Monazite, bastnasite. ~50% of world REE from carbonatites.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: After 2 half-lives, the percentage of the original radioactive parent isotope remaining is:
questions.solution: Fraction remaining = (1/2)^2 = 25%.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A bed drops 30 m over a horizontal distance of 20 m. Its dip angle is about:
questions.solution: dip = tan⁻¹(rise/run) = tan⁻¹(30/20) ≈ 56.3°.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A coal sample (proximate analysis) has 8% moisture, 25% volatile matter and 20% ash. Its fixed carbon is:
questions.solution: Fixed C = 100 − (moisture + VM + ash) = 100 − (8+25+20) = 47%.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Pump-and-treat remediation:
options: Extracts contaminated groundwater and treats it
questions.solution: Pump-and-treat: extract and treat at surface.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Isomorphism:
options: Different compositions, same crystal structure (e.g., olivine series)
questions.solution: Isomorphous series: Fe and Mg substitute in olivine (fayalite-forsterite).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Terraces:
options: Abandoned floodplain levels. Paired vs unpaired. Climatic or tectonic
questions.solution: Terraces: former floodplains. Paired: both sides same level (rapid incision).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Radar backscatter — smooth surface:
questions.solution: Rough surface: diffuse → high backscatter. Smooth: mirror-like → low return. Water: dark.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Geophysical method — resistivity:
options: Current injection. Vertical electrical sounding (VES) for groundwater
questions.solution: Resistivity: Wenner and Schlumberger arrays. Low ρ = water, clay. High ρ = hard rock.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Sequence stratigraphy: flooding surface vs maximum flooding surface:
options: FS: shallowing-upward break. MFS: deepest water, maximum landward shoreline
questions.solution: Flooding surface: parasequence boundary. MFS: maximum transgression. Condensed section.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Migmatite:
options: Partially melted metamorphic rock. Dark (restite) + light (leucosome) bands
questions.solution: Migmatite: between metamorphism and igneous. Leucosome: granitic melt.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Laterite ore deposit types:
options: Bauxite (Al), Ni-laterite (Ni), Fe (Fe), Mn (Mn). Weathering products
questions.solution: Laterite: tropical weathering. Al laterite: gibbsite, boehmite. Ni laterite: saprolite + limonite.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Maceral groups: vitrinite, inertinite, liptinite. Vitrinite originates from:
questions.solution: Vitrinite: from vascular plant tissues. Major maceral in most coals. Reflectance used for rank.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Aeolian landforms:
options: Barchan, transverse, star, parabolic dunes. Loess (wind-blown silt)
questions.solution: Barchan: crescent-shaped, horns downwind. Transverse: perpendicular to wind. Loess: fertile soil.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Porphyritic texture:
questions.solution: Porphyritic: two-stage cooling. Phenocrysts form slowly, groundmass quenches.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Garnet zoning profiles: bell-shaped Mn pattern indicates:
options: Prograde growth zoning (Mn partitioned into garnet early)
questions.solution: Garnet prograde: high Mn core, decreasing outward (Rayleigh fractionation). Growth zoning.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Quartz (hardness 7) will scratch, but not be scratched by, which mineral?
questions.solution: A mineral scratches anything softer; quartz (7) scratches feldspar (6) but not topaz/corundum/diamond.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Siwalik Group: Upper Siwalik (Plio-Pleistocene) facies:
questions.solution: Upper Siwalik: coarse alluvium. Middle: sandstone. Lower: fine sandstone and siltstone.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Crystal system of halite:
questions.solution: Halite (NaCl) — cubic crystals. Perfect cubic cleavage.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Gondwana basins in India:
questions.solution: Gondwana: rift basins. Lower Gondwana (Permian): coal measures. Upper Gondwana (Triassic-Jurassic): sandstones.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Radiometric correction:
options: Remove sensor and atmospheric effects. DN → radiance → reflectance. Calibration coefficients
questions.solution: Radiometric: from raw DN. Radiance: L = Gain·DN + Offset. Reflectance: ρ = π·L·d²/(ESUN·cosθ).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Soil moisture retrieval:
options: Passive microwave (L-band: SMOS, SMAP). Active (C-band: Sentinel-1). Optical/thermal
questions.solution: SMAP: L-band radiometer + radar. 3km, 2-3 day. Soil moisture + freeze/thaw. Surface ~5cm depth.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A bed dipping at 45° shows a width of 40 m across a horizontal surface. Its true (perpendicular) thickness is about:
questions.solution: True thickness = width × sin(dip) = 40×sin45° ≈ 28.28 m.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Coastal — spit:
options: Sand/gravel extension from headland. Hook at end. Longshore drift
questions.solution: Spit: continues until across a bay (baymouth bar). Tombolo: connects island to mainland.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Anderson's fault classification:
options: Normal fault: σ₁ vertical. Reverse: σ₃ vertical. Strike-slip: σ₂ vertical
questions.solution: Anderson: σ₁ = maximum principal stress. Orientation of fault depends on which stress is vertical.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A mineral specimen of mass 81 g has a volume of 30 cm³. Its specific gravity is about:
questions.solution: SG = mass/volume = 81/30 = 2.7.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Transmissivity T:
options: T = K·b (b = aquifer thickness). m²/day or m²/s
questions.solution: T: production capacity. T > 0.01 m²/s = good aquifer.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 3 m seam over 6 km² (density 1.4 t/m³) has a recovery factor of 0.6. The mineable reserve is about:
questions.solution: Mineable = area×thickness×density×recovery = 6M m²×3×1.4×0.6 ≈ 15.1 Mt.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In the faulted block shown, the hanging wall has moved down relative to the footwall. This is a:
questions.solution: Hanging wall down = normal fault, caused by extension/tension.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 260 130" width="260" height="130"><line x1="40" y1="25" x2="150" y2="80" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="2"/><rect x="20" y="40" width="120" height="70" fill="#fde68a" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1" transform="skewX(-10)"/><rect x="150" y="70" width="100" height="70" fill="#bfdbfe" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1"/><line x1="60" y1="55" x2="135" y2="92" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-dasharray="2 2"/><line x1="160" y1="90" x2="245" y2="90" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-dasharray="2 2"/><text x="190" y="82" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">hanging wall</text><text x="40" y="35" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">footwall</text></svg>
figure.caption: normal fault

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: On the Mohs scale of hardness, the hardest mineral (value 10) is:
questions.solution: Mohs scale runs from talc (1) to diamond (10), the hardest natural mineral.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Unconformity — paraconformity:
options: No visible erosional surface. Missing time recognized only by fossils
questions.solution: Paraconformity: parallel beds with no obvious erosion. Biostratigraphic gap.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: GIS coordinate systems: projected vs geographic:
options: Projected: flat (UTM, meters). Geographic: spherical (lat/lon, degrees)
questions.solution: Geographic: WGS84 lat/lon. Projected: UTM (meters). Distortion in projected systems.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Cement types in sandstone — paragenesis:
options: Early (calcite, pyrite) → Mesogenetic (quartz overgrowth, illite, ankerite) → Telogenetic (kaolinite, Fe-oxides)
questions.solution: Paragenesis: diagenetic sequence. Relative timing from cross-cutting and texture.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A rock retains 50% of its original parent isotope (half-life 0.7 Gyr). Its radiometric age is about:
questions.solution: 50% remaining ⇒ 1 half-lives ⇒ age = 1×0.7 = 0.7 Gyr.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An opencast block of 10000 m² has 30 m of overburden above the seam. The overburden volume to be removed is:
questions.solution: Volume = area × depth = 10000×30 = 300000 m³ = 0.3 million m³.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Vorticity analysis:
options: Porphyroclast rotation. Rigid grains in ductile matrix. Rotational vs non-rotational deformation
questions.solution: Vorticity number W_k: simple shear (1), pure shear (0), general (0-1). Hyperbolic flow.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Aquifer — water table:
options: Boundary between saturated and unsaturated zones. Fluctuates with recharge
questions.solution: Water table: follows topography. Perched water table: above regional water table.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Remote sensing for geology: lineament analysis identifies:
options: Faults, fractures, joints, dikes, lithological boundaries
questions.solution: Lineaments: linear features from satellite imagery. Structural control. Mineralization often associated.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The colour of a mineral's powder, seen when it is rubbed on a porcelain plate, is its:
questions.solution: Streak is the colour of the fine powder, often more diagnostic than the surface colour.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Pumping test — recovery:
options: Residual drawdown s' = (2.303Q)/(4πT)·log(t/t'). No S estimate needed
questions.solution: Recovery: T from slope of s' vs log(t/t'). More reliable than drawdown (constant rate).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Crystal symmetry elements: rotation axes, mirror planes, inversion centers:
options: Combination defines point group (32 crystallographic point groups)
questions.solution: Point group: symmetry operations. Space group: point group + translations = 230 groups.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Delta types:
questions.solution: Delta: river sediment deposition at coast. Tidal/wave influence shapes delta type.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: SEDEX (sedimentary exhalative):
options: Zn-Pb-Ag deposits in sedimentary basins. Exhalative on seafloor. Broken Hill, McArthur River
questions.solution: SEDEX: laminated ore. Reducing basin. Also large tonnage. Bagalkot (India).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Boninite: high-Mg, high-Si volcanic rock. Tectonic setting:
questions.solution: Boninite: MgO>8%, SiO₂>52%, TiO₂<0.5%. Forearc setting. High-T partial melting of hydrated mantle.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Mass extinction events: five major:
questions.solution: P-T extinction: 95% marine species. K-Pg: dinosaurs. End-Ordovician: glaciation.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Twinning — albite law:
options: Polysynthetic twinning in plagioclase. Diagnostic in thin section
questions.solution: Albite twinning: repeated lamellae. Characteristic of plagioclase feldspars.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Depositional environment — tidal flat:
options: Supratidal (marsh), intertidal (mudflat, sandflat), subtidal (channel). Herringbone cross-bedding, flaser bedding, lenticular bedding
questions.solution: Tidal: bipolar cross-bedding (ebb + flood). Mud drapes. Reactivation surfaces.
